name: rippling-flux-sdk

metadata.name: rippling-flux-sdk
metadata.summary: Defines the interfaces and data-models used by Rippling Flux Apps.

documentation:
# rippling-flux-sdk

Defines the interfaces and data-models used by Rippling Flux Apps.

The **distribution name** for this project is `rippling-flux-sdk`, which is how it is listed in PyPI. Once added as a
dependency though, use the name `flux_sdk` as the **import name**.

The imports for this package should be included in the scaffolding for a Flux App, in general Flux app developers will
not need to write that code. By being distributed as a package, it will allow your IDE to inspect the defined types for
additional context.


## Getting Started

Code is linted with ruff.  It's recommended to run `./install_hooks.sh` to run linting on commit.
Linting can also be run manually with `./run_ruff.sh`.  In many cases it's possible to auto-fix
linting errors with `./run_ruff.sh --fix`.

variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Field
documentation:
This represents the currently supported types for Record fields. Currently, we only support these primitive types and do
not allow for any complex/nested types.

variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Checkpoint
documentation:
Checkpoints can be represented as either timestamp (datetime), sequence number (int) or an opaque token (str). Values
are compared assuming an increasing sort order, so the largest value in a sync will be treated as the high-water mark
for the next sync.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.primary_key
documentation:
The ID for this record, which is derived from the Schema.primary_key_field and then becomes external_id in the
Custom Object. Since only a simple, string value is supported by Custom Objects, this does not need to be a full
SchemaField.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.fields
documentation: This is the rest of the raw data for this record.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.references
documentation:
This represents the links in this object, which is derived from Schema.references when reading the record data from
the source database. Each key is the field name, which is used to look up the target details. Each value is the
identifier used for the link.

For example, consider an Invoice object with a field named "customer_id" which is a foreign key pointing to another
object Customer via id:

```json
references = { "customer_id": "customer_1" }
```

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.checkpoint
documentation:
This is populated by the "process_records" hook to signal to Rippling the checkpoint that can be observed and
recorded after the sync is complete. Rippling will keep track of the *highest* value seen from all records. That
value will be passed to the "prepare_query" hook and can be used to adjust the query to only retrieve the records
that have changed since the last sync. When the checkpoint is used in the filtering, you should also sort the
records by the checkpoint, otherwise there will likely be records missed between syncs.

This must be used to enable incremental sync, otherwise a full sync will happen each time.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.record.Record.drop
documentation:
This flag can be used by the "process_records" hook to signal to Rippling that the object should not be imported.
If a Record is not found after this hook, that will be regarded as an error, so this flag should be used instead.
This corresponds to a row in the source database.

variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.SQLQueryArg
documentation: This is the reduced list of acceptable types that can be used as SQL arguments.

variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.Query
documentation: This is the list of types that can be used to represent a query.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.Connector
documentation: This enum can be used to communicate a connector type to app hooks.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.SQLQuery

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.SQLQuery.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.SQLQuery.text
documentation: This is the raw text of the query to be executed.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.SQLQuery.args
documentation:
This is used to add safe interpolation of values into the query, rather than requiring it to be constructed by the
hook. In the sql_query, each "@var" will be replaced with the corresponding "var" from this dict. The query text
must use only alphanumeric characters and underscores in variable names in order to be properly detected.

This is where a variable like "checkpoint" could be added to have it interpolated safely and cleanly.
This is returned by the "prepare_query" hook for SQL connectors.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery.collection
documentation: This indicates which collection the query will be executed in.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery.filter
documentation:
This provides the filter criteria for a basic find operation. Most connectors will use this to filter documents by
values or other simpler query operators.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery.projection
documentation: This provides the projection criteria for a basic find operation. Use this to include/exclude fields at query-time.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.query.MongoQuery.aggregate
documentation:
This allows for projection in advanced queries. Some connectors may offload more expensive operations (eg: join,
embed) to the database through this instead of using the basic filter.

This is returned by the "prepare_query" hook for MongoDB connectors. The inclusion of filter, projection and
aggregate support a variety of query patterns:

 - find: filter
 - find: filter + projection
 - find: projection
 - aggregate: pipeline

By opting for none of these optional parameters, the entire collection will be retrieved.

variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Reference
documentation: This lists the available types that can be used for schema references.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaDataType
documentation: These are the data types supported by Rippling Custom Objects.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.name
documentation: This maps to the field name in the Rippling Custom Object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.data_type
documentation: This indicates the data type used in the Rippling Custom Object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.description
documentation: This is a longer explanation of the field that will appear in the Rippling Custom Object UI.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.is_required
documentation: When enabled, this value will be required to be not empty by Rippling Custom Object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.is_unique
documentation: When enabled, this value will be enforced as unique by Rippling Custom Object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.enum_values
documentation: When using an Enum data type, this indicates the possible values.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.SchemaField.enum_restricted
documentation:
When using an Enum data type, this indicates if *only* enum_values are accepted, meaning anything else will be
rejected with an error.
A field usually corresponds to a column in a database, it is one datum within a Record.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.CustomObjectReference

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.CustomObjectReference.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.CustomObjectReference.object
documentation: The name of the Custom Object to link to.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.CustomObjectReference.lookup
documentation: The name of the field on the Custom Object to use for the link.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.CustomObjectReference.description
documentation: An optional explanation for this reference to be shown in the Rippling Custom Object UI.
This allows creating a reference to an existing Custom Object.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeLookup

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeLookup.EMPLOYEE_ID
documentation: The Rippling Employee ID.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeLookup.PERSONAL_EMAIL
documentation: The personal email for a Rippling Employee.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeLookup.WORK_EMAIL

These are the available lookup-fields for a Rippling Employee.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeReference

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeReference.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeReference.lookup
documentation: The name of the field on the Employee object to use for the link.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.EmployeeReference.description
documentation: An optional explanation for this reference to be shown in the Rippling Custom Object UI.
This allows creating a reference to a Rippling employee.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.name
documentation: This is the name of the Custom Object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.category_name
documentation:
This is used to put this Custom Object into a group/category. If a category with this name already exists, a new one
will not be created. If this value is changed, no categories will be deleted as they could be used by another
object.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.category_description
documentation:
When creating a new group/category, this will set a description that will appear in the Rippling Custom Object UI.
If the category already exists, the description will be updated.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.primary_key_field
documentation: This is the name of the field used for the primary key (aka: External ID).

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.name_field
documentation: This is the name of the field used for the record name, which is displayed in the Rippling Custom Object UI.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.fields
documentation: These are the remaining data fields.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.description
documentation: This is a more detailed explanation of the field which will appear in the Rippling Custom Object UI.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.created_date_field
documentation:
This is the name of the field that reflects when the record was first created. When a field name is not provided,
Rippling will use the time that the record is first imported.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.last_modified_date_field
documentation:
This is the name of the field that reflects when the record was last updated. When a field name is not provided,
Rippling will use the time that the record was last updated.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.references
documentation: These are the links to other objects. The keys are the field names that should be the origin for the link/edge.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.etl.data_models.schema.Schema.owner
documentation: This establishes the built-in link to Employee, if applicable.
A schema describes the shape of an object being imported and is used by Rippling to define the Custom Object.

classes.name: flux_sdk.etl.capabilities.single_object_import.interface.SingleObjectImport

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.capabilities.single_object_import.interface.SingleObjectImport.get_schema
documentation:
A function that defines schema for the records being imported.

Use this hook to indicate the structure (aka: schema) for the data being imported. This is used to ensure that
Rippling Custom Objects has a target for the extracted records to be loaded into.

In Custom Objects, fields are not able to change their data type once they are created. As a result, new fields
will be detected and created as expected. For existing fields, a change to a data type will result in an error,
while other metadata changes (eg: description, enum_values) will be applied. For the time-being, migration must
be performed by adding new fields.

```
return

Schema
```

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.capabilities.single_object_import.interface.SingleObjectImport.prepare_query
documentation:
A function that prepares the query that should be run by Rippling to extract records.

Use this hook to construct the query that should be run by Rippling to extract records. If the passed connector
is not supported by the hook, it should raise NotImplementedError to make the misconfiguration clear.

The "process_records" hook must decorate records with checkpoint in order to enable incremental sync.

```
param connector

This indicates what type of connector is configured, which may change the returned Query.

param schema

The schema generated in the "get_schema" hook for this object.

param checkpoint

If included, this is an incremental sync and the implementation should adjust the Query
```

accordingly to sort by the checkpoint.
\:return: Query

methods.name: flux_sdk.etl.capabilities.single_object_import.interface.SingleObjectImport.process_records
documentation:
A function that post process the results from `prepare_query` to fit in the schema.

Use this hook to post-process the Records extracted via the query performed from "prepare_query". Implementing
this method is required in order to implement incremental sync (by returning the checkpoint to Rippling) or to
dynamically drop or to dynamically drop records from the sync (by setting drop to True).

If the Record does not adhere to the provided schema, such as using an incorrect/incompatible type for a field,
the sync will fail. As such, this hook has the opportunity to massage any raw query results that may not be
directly compatible.

```
param schema

The schema generated in the "get_schema" hook for this object.

param records

The batch of records to be updated

return

Records
```

Extract, transform, and load data into a Rippling Custom Object from anywhere.

This class represents the "single_object_import" capability. This allows the import of a single object type from a
database source.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.Organization

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.Organization.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the organization in the third party system.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.Organization.name
documentation: The name of the organization in the third party system.

This represents an organization in the third party system.
An organization is used to group users in the third party system.
Different third party systems may have different names of organizations:
Github calls it "Organization", while Google Workspace calls it "Domain".

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User.id
documentation:
The unique identifier of the user in the third party system.
This field is required when getting users from the third party system.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User.first_name
documentation: The first name of the user.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User.middle_name
documentation: The middle name of the user.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User.last_name
documentation: The last name of the user.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.User.email
documentation: The email address of the user.
This represents a user in the third party system.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetUsersRequest

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetUsersRequest.organizations
documentation:
The organizations to get users from. If this field is not provided, 
the users from all organizations should be returned.
This represents a request to get users from the third party system.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetUsersResponse

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetUsersResponse.users
documentation: The users from the third party system.
This represents a response containing the users from the third party system.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetOrganizationsRequest
documentation: This represents a request to get organizations from the third party system.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetOrganizationsResponse

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.user_management.data_models.data_models.GetOrganizationsResponse.organizations
documentation: The organizations from the third party system.
This represents a response containing the organizations from the third party system.

classes.name: flux_sdk.user_management.capabilities.user_lifecycle_management.interface.UserLifecycleManagement

methods.name: flux_sdk.user_management.capabilities.user_lifecycle_management.interface.UserLifecycleManagement.get_users
documentation:
A function that get users from the third-party system.

Use this hook fetch users from the third-party system so that they can be matched with employees in Rippling.
\:param request: The request to get users from the third-party system. This request may contain the
organizations to get users from.
\:return: The response containing the users from the third-party system.

methods.name: flux_sdk.user_management.capabilities.user_lifecycle_management.interface.UserLifecycleManagement.get_organizations
documentation:
A function that get organizations from the third-party system.

Use this hook to fetch organizations from the third-party system so that the organization information can be
used to get users from the third-party system.
\:param request: The request to get organizations from the third-party system.
\:return: The response containing the organizations from the third-party system.

This class represents the "user_lifecycle_management" capability.
This capability is used to manage the lifecycle of users in the third-party system with supports including:
- Getting user's organizations from the third-party system
- Getting users from the third-party system

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.interface.JobManagement.get_job_attributes
documentation:
A function that gets job attributes from the third-party system.

Use this hook to fetch job attributes from the third-party system
so that they can be matched with attributes in Rippling.

If request.requested_attribute_values is False, only fetch the mapping of the attributes to Rippling.

If request.requested_attribute_values is True, fetch the mapping and the attribute values.

Only fetch data for the attributes in request.requested_attributes. If requested_attributes is None, fetch all.
Note, it is recommended to use the key_in_requested_attributes helper in the requested.

```
param request

The request containing the attributes to fetch and if values are required.

return

The response containing the job attributes from the third-party system, mapping to their Rippling
```

attributes and possible values.

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.interface.JobManagement.get_employees_pay_rate_overrides
documentation:
A function that gets the pay rate override for each employee that requires an override.

Use this hook to fetch the pay rate override for each employee that requires it.

The pay rate override is tied to the attribute mapped to RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE.

The pay rate override will take precedence over the default pay rate returned from the get_job_attributes hook.

```
return

The response containing the attribute id that maps to pay rate mapping to a list of pay rate overrides.
```

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.interface.TimeEntryManagement

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.interface.TimeEntryManagement.fetch_time_entries
documentation:
Fetch all time entries for a given query filter.

```
param query

The query filter to fetch time entries.

return

A list of time entries that match the query.
```

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.interface.TimeEntryManagement.get_break_types
documentation:
A function that gets the break types defined in the third party system.

Use this hook to fetch the break types so that the customer can map them to the break types in Rippling.

When importing the type entries, the breaks should contain the break type id, which can be resolved to the
break type in Rippling.

```
return

The response containing all the break types available in the third party system.
```

Sync time entry related data from 3rd party to Rippling

This class represents the TimeEntryManagement capability. The developer is supposed to implement
fetch_time_entries and fetch_break_types in their implementation.

An instance of this class cannot be initiated unless both methods are implemented.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntriesQuery

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntriesQuery.start_time
documentation: start_time: This field denotes the time entry start datetime with timezone (after) to filter for.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntriesQuery.end_time
documentation: end_time: This field denotes the time entry end datetime with timezone (before) to filter for.

This class unifies supported queries for time entries data. Not all app APIs may support
all of these filters, it is up to the app developer to handle those cases and provide the
correct time entry list that matches the query.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift

documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift.id
documentation: id: This field denotes the 3rd party job shift id.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift.job_attributes
documentation:
job_attributes: This dict denotes the 3rd party job identifying attribute key-values that will be 
provided with the JobShift. Non string attribute values should be serialized to strings.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift.start_time
documentation: start_time: This field denotes the job shift start datetime with timezone.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift.end_time
documentation: end_time: This field denotes the job shift end datetime with timezone.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.JobShift.description
documentation: description: This field denotes the 3rd party job shift description.

This class represents the equivalent of a Job Shift period. It may be called different
names in different apps, but should reflect clocked in periods. Note that these may overlap
in time with breaks but must not overlap with other JobShifts.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.id
documentation: id: This field denotes the 3rd party break id.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.start_time
documentation: start_time: This field denotes the break start datetime with timezone.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.end_time
documentation: end_time: This field denotes the break end datetime with timezone.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.description
documentation: description: This field denotes the 3rd party break description.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.Break.break_type_id
documentation: break_type_id: This field denotes the id of the break type that's associated with the break.

This class represents the equivalent of a break duration. It may be called different
names in different apps, but should reflect break periods. Note that these may overlap
in time with JobShifts but must not overlap with other Breaks.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.BreakType

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.BreakType.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.BreakType.id
documentation: id: This field denotes the 3rd party break type id.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.BreakType.name
documentation: name: This field denotes the break type name.

This class represents the equivalent of a break type. It may be called different
names in different apps, but should reflect the type of the break in the third party
system so that it can be mapped to the correct break type in Rippling by the customer.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.GetBreakTypesResponse

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.GetBreakTypesResponse.__post_init__

documentation: break_types: This field denotes the list of 3rd party break types.
This class represents the response of the get_break_types hook.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.id
documentation: id: This field denotes the 3rd party time entry id.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.user_id
documentation: user_id: This field denotes the 3rd party user id this time entry belongs to.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.job_shifts
documentation: job_shifts: This field denotes the list of 3rd party job shifts this time entry contains.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.breaks
documentation: breaks: This field denotes the list of 3rd party breaks this time entry contains.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.start_time
documentation: start_time: This field denotes the time entry start datetime with timezone.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.time_entry_management.data_models.TimeEntry.end_time
documentation: end_time: This field denotes the break end datetime with timezone.

This class represents the equivalent of overarching time entry object. It may be called different
names in different apps, but should reflect the overarching record that tracks time segments of
work and breaks as child lists, but should also have its own start and end times to reflect the
period of time the time entry covers.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.RipplingAttribute
documentation:
This represents the supported attributes in Rippling.
Your third-party system attributes should be mapped to one or more of these attributes.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.PayRateCompatibleValue

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.PayRateCompatibleValue.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.PayRateCompatibleValue.pay_rate
documentation: The pay rate associated with the job title, str with max 4 decimals, for ex, 43.3943
This represents a pay rate compatible value.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.AttributeValue

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.AttributeValue.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.AttributeValue.id
documentation:
The unique id associated with the attribute value.
For ex, the id for a particular job title in the third party system.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.AttributeValue.name
documentation:
The unique value associated with the attribute. Please strip any whitespaces.
For ex, if the attribute was "job_title", the value could be "Cashier".

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.AttributeValue.associated_attribute_values
documentation:
The non-empty list of associated attribute values based on compatible_rippling_attributes.
For ex, if the attribute was "job_title" and compatible_rippling_attributes was [PAY_RATE], 
the associated_attribute_values should be [PayRateCompatibleValue(...)].

If the compatible_rippling_attributes was [WORK_LOCATION] or [JOB_SITE_LOCATION] (or both), 
the associated_attribute_values could be [AddressCompatibleValue(...)].

This represents an attribute value associated with an attribute.

Each attribute value will have an id, a name, and a list of associated attribute values based on the
compatible_rippling_attributes field.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.id
documentation: The unique id associated with the attribute, for ex, job_title.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.name
documentation: The unique name associated with the attribute, for ex, "Job title". Please strip any whitespaces.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.description
documentation: The optional description associated with the attribute.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.compatible_rippling_attributes
documentation:
The non-empty list of Rippling attributes that are compatible with the attribute, 
sorted from most compatible to least, for ex, [PAY_RATE].

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.Attribute.attribute_values
documentation:
The non-empty (if not None) list of attribute values associated with the attribute.
This field should only be used if requested_attribute_values is True in GetJobAttributesRequest.
Note that attribute name should be unique across attribute_values.

This represents an attribute from the third party system and how it is mapped to Rippling.

Attribute values should also be provided if requested by Rippling.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesRequest

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesRequest.key_in_requested_attributes
documentation:
Check if the key is in the requested attributes.
Returns True if key exists, or if requested_attributes is None.

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesRequest.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesRequest.requested_attribute_values
documentation:
Whether to fetch the values of the attributes or not.
If False, GetJobAttributesResponse will only contain the list of attributes and how they map to Rippling.
If True, GetJobAttributesResponse will also contain the values of the attributes in addition to their mappings.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesRequest.requested_attributes
documentation:
The third party attributes to fetch the values for. If None, all attributes should be fetched.
Note, it is recommended to use the key_in_requested_attributes helper.

This represents a request to fetch the attributes from the third party system, how they map to Rippling,
and optionally their values.

If we require the values of the attributes, requested_attribute_values will be True.

We may also only request information about certain attributes through requested_attributes.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesResponse

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesResponse.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetJobAttributesResponse.attributes
documentation:
The non-empty list of attributes from the third party system and how they map to Rippling.
The value of the attributes may also be requested.

This represents a response containing the attributes from the third party system, how they map to Rippling,
and optionally their values.

See GetJobAttributesRequest to determine if values are requested, and which attributes are requested.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.EmployeePayRateOverride

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.EmployeePayRateOverride.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.EmployeePayRateOverride.employee_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the user in the third party system.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.EmployeePayRateOverride.attribute_value_id
documentation:
The unique id of the attribute value for this employee.
For ex, if the attribute_id was "job_title" and the job title for this employee is "Cashier", the attribute_value_id
 would be the id associated with "Cashier", for ex "123".
 
 Note, if an employee maps to this attribute_value_id, it should not be mapped again within the same attribute_id.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.EmployeePayRateOverride.pay_rate
documentation: The pay rate associated with the attribute_value_id, a str with max 4 decimals, for ex, 43.3943.

This represents the pay rate override for a specific employee, tied to the attribute_id that maps to
 RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE.

For instance, assume that third party attribute_id job_title is mapped to RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE.

Here are some example data:

EmployeePayRateOverride(employee_id="123", pay_rate="43.3943", attribute_value_id="123")

If a pay rate is specified in the get_job_attributes hook, we will override if an employee has a specific override.

Note that an employee can have more than one override as long as the attribute_value_id is unique.

classes.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetEmployeesPayRateOverridesResponse

methods.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetEmployeesPayRateOverridesResponse.__post_init__
documentation: Perform validation.

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.time_and_attendance.capabilities.job_management.data_models.GetEmployeesPayRateOverridesResponse.employee_pay_rate_overrides_per_attribute
documentation:
The dict which maps attribute_id with the pay rate overrides associated with the employees for this attribute_id.

The key should be the attribute_id that directly maps to RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE, 
as determined in the get_job_attributes hook.

ex: {"job_title": [EmployeePayRateOverride(...),...]}

For now, we only support one attribute_id, which is the attribute_id that maps to RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE.
    
We will determine the employee pay rate based on the pay rate value specified in the get_job_attributes hook,
 if it is not overriden in the above mapping.

This represents a response containing the pay rates overrides for employees.

Employees pay rate overrides are tied to a specific attribute_id that maps to RipplingAttribute.PAY_RATE.

If a pay rate value is specified in the get_job_attributes hook, we will update if an employee has an override.

For now, we only support overriding pay rates for one attribute_id.

classes.name: flux_sdk.fertility.capabilities.report_eligibility_data.interface.ReportEligibilityData

methods.name: flux_sdk.fertility.capabilities.report_eligibility_data.interface.ReportEligibilityData.format_eligibility_file
documentation:
Given a list of EmployeeEligibilityRecord, prepare a file for upload to the provider.

The file will be sent verbatim, so any compression or other formatting required by the provider
must be applied within this function.
\:param company_info:
\:param employee_eligibility_records:
\:return:

Report eligibility data for employees in your application via data in Rippling.

This class represents the "report eligibility data" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
format_eligibility_file in their implementation.

An instance of this class cannot be initiated unless this method is implemented.

classes.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.update_deduction_elections.data_models.EmployeeDeductionSetting

class_variables.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.update_deduction_elections.data_models.EmployeeDeductionSetting.client_id
documentation: The client identifier to be sent if available.

classes.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.update_deduction_elections.interface.UpdateDeductionElections

methods.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.update_deduction_elections.interface.UpdateDeductionElections.parse_deductions
documentation:
A function that takes a stream and returns a list of EmployeeDeductionSetting objects.

This method receives a stream from which the developer is expected to return a list of EmployeeDeductionSetting
for each employee identifier (SSN).
\:param uri: Contains the path of file
\:param stream: Contains the stream
\:return: list[EmployeeDeductionSetting]

Update deduction elections for employees in Rippling via data in your application.

This class represents the "update deduction elections" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
parse_deductions or parse_deduction method in their implementation. For further details regarding their
implementation details, check their documentation.

A instance of this class cannot be initiated unless either of these 2 methods are implemented.

classes.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.report_payroll_contributions.interface.ReportPayrollContributions

methods.name: flux_sdk.pension.capabilities.report_payroll_contributions.interface.ReportPayrollContributions.format_contributions
documentation:
A function that takes a list of EmployeePayrollRecord and PayrollUploadSettings and returns a formatted file.

Given a list of EmployeePayrollRecord and the PayrollUploadSettings, prepare a file for upload to the pension
provider. The file will be sent verbatim, so any compression or other formatting required by the pension
provider must be applied within this function.
\:param employee_payroll_records:
\:param payroll_upload_settings:
\:return: File

Report payroll contributions for employees in your application via data in Rippling.

This class represents the "report payroll contribution" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
format_contributions method in their implementation. For further details regarding their
implementation details, check their documentation.

not present please reach out to apps@rippling.com to add support for that attribute.

classes.name: flux_sdk.flux_core.data_models.ContributionType
documentation:
This enum is an exhaustive list of all 401k Contribution types supported by Rippling. Developers are expected
to use this to fetch the Employee deduction contribution based on contribution type. If a contribution type your app
requires is not present please reach out to apps@rippling.com to add support for that particular deduction.

classes.name: flux_sdk.flux_core.data_models.DeductionType
documentation:
This enum is an exhaustive list of all 401k deduction types supported by Rippling. Developers are expected
to create EmployeeDeductionSetting with deduction_type being among this list. If a deduction your app requires is
not present please reach out to apps@rippling.com to add support for that particular deduction.

classes.name: flux_sdk.flux_core.data_models.PayrollRunType
documentation:
This enum is an exhaustive list of all payroll run types supported by Rippling. Developers are expected to use this
to make decisions for file attributes based on run type.

classes.name: flux_sdk.benefits_administration.capabilities.report_employees_hr_data.interface.ReportEmployeesHrData

methods.name: flux_sdk.benefits_administration.capabilities.report_employees_hr_data.interface.ReportEmployeesHrData.format_employees_hr_data
documentation:
This hook is for transforming the employee HR data into a file will be sent to the partner company.

This method receives the app's configuration data and a list of employee data.
The developer is expected to create a file, formated to their use case, and return that file.
The file will be tranfered to the partner company via SFTP

```
param config

Employee's HR data configuration

param employee_data

List of employee data

return

File
```

This capability offers developers a dataset containing personal and employment information for every employee
within a company.

This class represents the "report employee data" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
the following methods in their implementation. 

The methods on this class are required to instantiate an instance of this class.

classes.name: flux_sdk.flex.capabilities.update_enrollments.interface.UpdateEnrollments

methods.name: flux_sdk.flex.capabilities.update_enrollments.interface.UpdateEnrollments.get_formatted_enrollments_files
documentation:
A function that converts employee enrollments to formatted enrollment file.

This method receives company information and a list of EmployeeEnrollment objects. The developer is expected to
return the app specific list of formatted enrollment files.
\:param company_info:
\:param employee_enrollments:
\:param app_context:
\:return:

This class represents the "update enrollments" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
get_formatted_enrollment_files in their implementation. For further details regarding their
implementation details, check their documentation.

An instance of this class cannot be initiated unless this method is implemented.

classes.name: flux_sdk.flex.capabilities.update_contributions.interface.UpdateContributions

methods.name: flux_sdk.flex.capabilities.update_contributions.interface.UpdateContributions.get_formatted_contributions_files
documentation:
A function that converts employee contributions to formatted contribution file.

This method receives company information and a list of EmployeeContribution objects. The developer is expected
to return the app specific list of formatted contribution files.
\:param company_info:
\:param employee_contributions:
\:param app_context:
\:return:

Report employee payroll contributions for contribution types (FSA, HSA, etc.) in a payroll run to a benefit
 provider.

This class represents the "update contributions" capability. The developer is supposed to implement
get_formatted_enrollment_files in their implementation. For further details regarding their
implementation details, check their documentation.

An instance of this class cannot be initiated unless this method is implemented.
